title: Meta block access: tests
analysis:
Task 213 adds end-to-end (E2E) tests for the meta block access feature implemented in tasks 211 (compiler) and 212 (evaluator). The existing test coverage is split across unit/boundary layers:

- **Compiler tests** (`crates/reify-compiler/tests/meta_compile_tests.rs`, 6 tests): meta storage, MetaAccess compilation, nonexistent key error, no meta block error, duplicate error, constraint context. These test compilation only.
- **Expr eval tests** (`crates/reify-expr/tests/meta_eval_tests.rs`, 4 tests): MetaAccess evaluation with mock contexts. These test the expression evaluator in isolation.
- **Eval integration tests** (`crates/reify-eval/tests/meta_eval_integration.rs`, 6 tests): Let binding resolution, sub-structure access, edit_param stability, guarded groups, collection subs, cached eval. These use `TopologyTemplateBuilder` (no parsing).

**Gap:** No tests exercise the full pipeline from `.ri` source text through parse → compile → eval → verify values. This is what task 213 provides — E2E integration tests proving the feature works end-to-end with real source code.

The E2E pattern is well-established in the codebase (`occurrence_eval.rs`, `e2e_bracket.rs`): parse with `reify_syntax::parse()`, compile with `reify_compiler::compile()`, eval with `Engine::new(MockConstraintChecker, None).eval()`, then assert on `result.values`.

All 8 required scenarios map naturally to this pattern. Error cases (nonexistent key, no meta block) verify compile-time diagnostics without needing eval. Success cases verify runtime values. String comparison is tested through let bindings (`let ok : Bool = meta.key == "expected"`) rather than constraints, because `SimpleConstraintChecker` doesn't wire meta context into its EvalContext — a known limitation that doesn't affect the feature correctness.

steps.description: Create `crates/reify-eval/tests/meta_e2e.rs` with a helper function `parse_compile_eval(source) -> (CompiledModule, EvalResult)` that runs the full pipeline (parse → compile → eval with MockConstraintChecker). Write test `e2e_meta_access_on_structure`: source has `structure def Bracket { meta { description = "A bracket" } let label : String = meta.description }`, verify label == Value::String("A bracket"). Should pass immediately since tasks 211/212 implemented the feature.

Write test `e2e_meta_access_on_occurrence`: source has `occurrence def Welding { meta { process = "MIG" } let label : String = meta.process }`, verify entity_kind == EntityKind::Occurrence and label == Value::String("MIG"). This is the ONLY test covering meta blocks on occurrences — no existing test uses `occurrence def` with meta.

Write test `e2e_meta_nonexistent_key_error`: source has `structure def X { meta { a = "1" } let x : String = meta.nonexistent }`, parse and compile (no eval), assert at least one error diagnostic containing "no key". Validates compile-time key validation through the full pipeline.

Write test `e2e_meta_no_meta_block_error`: source has `structure def X { param width : Length = 10mm  let x : String = meta.foo }`, parse and compile (no eval), assert at least one error diagnostic containing "no meta block". Validates compile-time meta block presence check through the full pipeline.

Write test `e2e_meta_on_sub_structure`: source defines two structures — `structure def Part { meta { material = "steel" } let label : String = meta.material  param size : Length = 10mm }` and `structure def Assembly { sub part = Part() }`. After eval, verify Part's label == Value::String("steel"). Proves child template meta resolves correctly when evaluated as part of a parent assembly.

Write test `e2e_meta_value_in_let_binding`: source has `structure def Widget { meta { author = "Team A", version = "2.0", material = "steel" }  let a : String = meta.author  let v : String = meta.version  let m : String = meta.material }`. After eval, verify all three let bindings resolve to their respective meta values. Tests multiple keys accessed via multiple let bindings.

Write test `e2e_meta_string_comparison_equal`: source has `structure def Bolt { meta { grade = "A2" }  let matches : Bool = meta.grade == "A2" }`. After eval, verify matches == Value::Bool(true). Proves meta values participate correctly in string equality comparisons at runtime.

Write test `e2e_meta_string_comparison_not_equal`: source has `structure def Bolt { meta { grade = "A2" }  let matches : Bool = meta.grade == "A4" }`. After eval, verify matches == Value::Bool(false). Proves string comparison correctly reports inequality when meta value differs from the compared literal.

design_decisions.decision: All tests use E2E pipeline (parse → compile → eval) rather than builder APIs
design_decisions.rationale: Tasks 211/212 already provide unit and boundary tests using builder APIs. Task 213's value is proving the feature works with real .ri source code through the complete pipeline. This catches integration issues between parser, compiler, and evaluator that layer-specific tests miss.

design_decisions.decision: String comparison tested via let bindings, not constraints
design_decisions.rationale: SimpleConstraintChecker creates EvalContext without meta context (EvalContext::new(values, functions) — no .with_meta()). MetaAccess in a constraint expression would panic at runtime with SimpleConstraintChecker. MockConstraintChecker doesn't evaluate expressions at all. Testing `let matches : Bool = meta.key == "literal"` achieves the same coverage since the Engine's eval() properly wires meta into EvalContext for let binding evaluation.

design_decisions.decision: Use MockConstraintChecker for all E2E tests
design_decisions.rationale: The MockConstraintChecker returns Satisfied by default without evaluating expressions. This avoids the meta-context gap in SimpleConstraintChecker while still testing the eval pipeline. Constraints are not the focus of these tests — meta value resolution is.

All tests follow the same E2E pattern and have the same dependencies (reify-syntax, reify-compiler, reify-eval, reify-test-support). Placing them in one file with a shared helper minimizes boilerplate. The crate's Cargo.toml already has all needed dev-dependencies.

design_decisions.decision: Sub-structure test verifies child's own meta, not parent accessing child's meta
design_decisions.rationale: In the compiler, `meta.key` syntax resolves to the current entity's meta (scope.entity_name). There's no syntax for cross-entity meta access like `sub_name.meta.key`. The E2E test proves the child template's meta let-bindings resolve correctly when the child is instantiated as a sub-component of a parent.

reuse.what: E2E test pattern: parse → compile → eval → verify
reuse.how: Follow the same helper pattern: reify_syntax::parse() → reify_compiler::compile() → Engine::new(MockConstraintChecker, None).eval() → assert on result.values. Extract into a shared helper fn.
Returns Satisfied for all constraints by default. Used by all non-geometry E2E tests in the codebase.
Construct cell IDs to look up evaluated values in result.values HashMap.
Filter diagnostics by Severity::Error and assert on error message content for compile-error tests.
